title: erigeron suksdorfii
intro: Erigeron suksdorfii, also known as Drouet's fleabane or Suksdorf's daisy, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and prevent infection. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antacid. Erigeron suksdorfii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
